videos.title: Length Word Problems | Solving Measurement Problems Step by Step
videos.description: Solve fun word problems about measuring length! Learn how to add, subtract, and compare lengths in real-life situations like measuring cloth and roads.
learningObjectives: Read and understand length word problems
Identify which operation to use (add, subtract, compare)
Solve step by step with correct units

videos.title: Converting Centimetres to Metres | Length Conversion Made Easy
videos.description: Learn how to convert centimetres to metres and metres to centimetres. Remember: 1 metre = 100 centimetres. Practise with step-by-step examples!
learningObjectives: Review conversions needed for word problems
Convert within a word problem context

videos.title: Metres to Kilometres | Converting Bigger Units of Length
videos.description: Learn how to convert metres to kilometres and kilometres to metres. Remember: 1 kilometre = 1000 metres. Great examples with roads and distances!
learningObjectives: Review m to km conversion for distance problems
Work with larger numbers in measurement
keyConceptsCovered: Converting m to km in word problems

videos.title: Adding and Subtracting Bigger Numbers | Addition and Subtraction Review
videos.description: Review adding and subtracting numbers up to 1000. You need these skills to solve length problems where you add or subtract measurements!
learningObjectives: Handle carrying and borrowing in measurement context

videos.title: Estimating Lengths | Guess and Measure for Kids
videos.description: Learn how to estimate the length of objects before measuring them. Use your hand span, arm length, and footsteps to make smart guesses!
learningObjectives: Use estimation to check word problem answers
Decide if an answer is reasonable
Estimate before solving to plan the solution

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Word problems with conversion review for strong students

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete word problem coverage with estimation for checking answers

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Arithmetic review first, then conversion review, then word problems

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites

questions.question: Raju walks 2 km to school and 2 km back home. How far does he walk in total?
questions.explanation: Raju walks 2 km to school + 2 km back = 4 km in total. We add the two distances together.

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Use real-life scenarios from the students' daily lives: distance from home to school, length of the playground, height of the classroom door. Have students create their own word problems.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: When travelling, ask your child to estimate distances and solve simple problems: 'We drove 15 km to the market and 8 km to grandma's house. How far did we drive in total?'
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Word problems are like puzzles! Read carefully, find the numbers, decide whether to add or subtract, and always write the unit (cm, m, or km) in your answer.
